# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kafka broker configuration file (server.properties) for production deployment. Configure listeners, log directories, and replication settings. Set up Zookeeper connection and broker ID. This is for our distributed Kafka cluster.
# Category: kafka_topic_authorization

# server.properties (Kafka Broker Configuration file - Production Deployment) 

broker.id=0 # Unique id of the brokr, can be any integer or string , default is random number in case if you don't provide it manually  
listeners: PLAINTEXT://localhost:9092 (listening to all IPs and port 9092)   
advertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://your_server_ip:port # Listener addresses for external consumption of the Kafka cluster  
num.network.threads= 3 num.io.threads = 8 replication.factor =1,brokerA : 2 brokerB is a replica set with all brokers in another data center and they listen on port '9094' (default), zookeeper=zookeeper_host:port for managing the cluster id  
log.dirs:/path/to/data # Log dir where logs are stored   
log.retention.minutes = 15, log.retention.hours = -10 replication-allocation.[replicaA].protocol=PLAINTEXT replica-alloc[..] .brokerB:2 brokerC is a set of 3 replicas listening on different ports   ## Defaults are PLAINTEXT (only one) for all brokers and use 'localhost' as their listen address.
